Title: Open Source Friday with Kong's OpenMeter
Author: GitHub
Description:
In this Open Source Friday episode, Teja from Kong explains Kong’s role as an API gateway and AI connectivity platform, then introduces OpenMeter, an open source project focused on usage-based metering and billing that became part of Kong in September 2025. He demos Kong Connect’s cloud UI by creating a serverless gateway to proxy a sample flights API, adding key authentication, creating a consumer, and then enabling metering and billing. The conversation covers why usage tracking is difficult at scale, including token-based AI pricing, mentions OpenMeter’s SDK for integrating with custom services, and shares ways to contribute via the OpenMeter repo, Kong community Slack, docs, and a blog post about monetizing an AI agent.

00:00 Welcome and Chat Setup 01:33 Meet Tesia at Kong 02:32 What Kong Does 04:20 Why OpenMeter Exists 08:14 Connect Cloud Demo Overview 09:38 Gateway and Route Setup 11:26 Add Auth and Consumer 13:04 Enable Metering and Pricing 17:12 Plans Subscriptions Invoices 20:32 Real World Use Cases 24:27 Contribute and Community 26:38 Blog Story and Wrap Up
